title: Kendrickia walkeri
intro: Kendrickia walkeri, also known as the Indian snakeroot, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its tall, herbaceous stems and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and modulating immune responses. The bioactive constituents of Kendrickia walkeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Kendrickia walkeri is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Kendrickia walkeri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Tiliaceae. It is a plant native to Australia. Kendrickia walkeri belongs to the genus Kendrickia, which is a monotypic genus. This means that Kendrickia walkeri is the only species within the Kendrickia genus. The classification of Kendrickia walkeri is still under review, with some sources placing it in the family Malvaceae.
